revision.text:
[[en:Aoshima Shrine]][[fr:Aoshima-jinja]][[ja:青島神社]][[simple:Aoshima Shrine]] {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Aoshima-jinja
青島神社 | image = File:Aoshima jinja, Worship Hall 01.jpg | caption = {{Infobox mapframe|tyep=shape|zoom=14|frame-width=220|qq=}} | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|31.804667|N|131.474861|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Hoori]]
{{ill|Toyotama-hime|en|Toyotama-hime|qid=Q2329261|lt=Otohime|1=Toyotama-hime}}
''Shiotsuchi-no-oji'' | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = 2-13-1, Aoshima, [[Miyazaki, Miyazaki|Miyazaki]]
{{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|en|Miyazaki Prefecture|qid=Q130300|lt=Miyazaki|1=Miyazaki Prefecture}} 889-2162 | website = {{URL|http://www9.ocn.ne.jp/~aosima/|qq=}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name = Aoshima Shrine | location = 2-13-1 Aoshima, [[:en:Miyazaki City|Miyazaki City]], {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|en|Miyazaki Prefecture|qid=Q130300|lt=Miyazaki Prefecture|1=Miyazaki Prefecture}} | ISO = JP-45 | type = 31|48|16.8|N | longitude = 131|28|29.5|E | festival Deities = Hikohohodemi no Mikoto
Toyotama-hime no Mikoto
Shiotsuchi no Ōkami | Shrine Rank = Former [[Murasha]]
[[Beppyo Shrine]] | established = Unknown | main hall = Nagare-zukuri copper roof | alternate names = | Pilgrimage Sites = Hyuga no Kuni Shichifukujin Reijo | festival = October 18 | ceremony = Shima-biraki festival (Lunisolar March 16)
Summer festival (Maritime festival) (Lunisolar June 17)
Hadaka-mairi (Coming of Age Day) | map = Japan Miyazaki | location = {{ill|Q117462824|qid=Q117462824|1=Aoshima}} }} {{nihongo|'''Aoshima Shrine'''|青島神社|Aoshima jinja|qq=}} is a Shinto shrine located on {{ill|Aoshima, Miyazaki|en|Aoshima, Miyazaki|qid=Q4778779|lt=Aoshima|1=Aoshima, Miyazaki}} Island, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|en|Miyazaki Prefecture|qid=Q130300|lt=Miyazaki Prefecture|1=Miyazaki Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}.{{Cite web |last= |first= |title=Aoshima-jinja Shrine {{!|qq=}} Travel Japan - Japan National Tourism Organization (Official Site) |url=https://www.japan.travel/en/spot/632/ |access-date=2024-02-14 |website=Travel Japan |language=en-us|qq=}}{{Cite book |last1=Sharpley |first1=Richard |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=yA8HEAAAQBAJ |title=Tourism Development in Japan: Themes, Issues and Challenges |last2=Kato |first2=Kumi |date=2020-10-29 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-000-20561-9 |pages=105 |language=en|qq=}} It is dedicated to [[:en:Hohodemi|Hikohohodemi]], {{ill|Toyotama-hime|en|Toyotama-hime|qid=Q2329261|qid=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|1=Toyotama-hime}} and Shiozuchi-no-ōkami''.'' ==Overview== [[File:Aoshima Island Aerial photograp 2017.jpg|thumb|Aoshima (2017 aerial photo)
{{National Land Aerial Photograph|qq=}}.]] '''Aoshima Shrine''' (あおしまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Aoshima|en|Aoshima|qid=Q616108|lt=Aoshima|1=Aoshima}}, [[:en:Miyazaki City|Miyazaki City]], {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|en|Miyazaki Prefecture|qid=Q130300|lt=Miyazaki Prefecture|1=Miyazaki Prefecture}}. It is situated almost in the center of [[Aoshima Island]], which has a circumference of 1.5 km, and the entire island serves as its precincts. The former [[shrine Rank]] is [[Murasha]], and it is currently a [[Beppyo Shrine]] under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. The entire island of Aoshima is designated as a Special Natural Monument of Japan due to its tropical and subtropical plant communities. == festival Deities == The shrine enshrines Hikohohodemi no Mikoto (also known as Amatsu Hida Hikohohodemi no Mikoto), his wife Toyotama-hime no Mikoto, and Shiotsuchi no Ōkami. These deities are associated with the [[legend of Yamasachihiko and Umisachihiko]], and they are worshipped for matchmaking, safe childbirth, and maritime safety. == History == Originally, the shrine was established due to the faith in the sea‘Japanese Gods’.. Since ancient times, Aoshima itself has been revered as a sacred area[http://www.miyazaki-city.tourism.or.jp/tourism/spot/12.html Miyazaki City Tourism Association, "Aoshima Shrine"] (accessed November 30, 2010)., and until the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, the entire island was considered off-limits. According to shrine tradition, Hikohohodemi no Mikoto landed on Aoshima upon returning from the [[Watatsumi Palace]], and established a palace there. The shrine was built on the site of this palace, enshrining him along with Toyotama-hime no Mikoto and Shiotsuchi no ŌkamiIt is also said that Toyotama-hime no Mikoto built a birth hut in the cave of Udo (now [[Udo Shrine]]) when she gave birth.. However, detailed records of its founding were lost in the {{ill|Kanbun|en|Kanbun|qid=Q7901319|lt=Kanbun|1=Kanbun}} 2 (1662) earthquake‘Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Annals’.. According to the shrine’s records, it is mentioned in an ancient text from the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}} titled ‘Hyuuga Miyage’ (Gifts from Hyuga) that the shrine was already revered during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Saga|en|Saga|qid=Q180494|lt=Saga|1=Saga}}. The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|Bunki|en|Bunki|qid=Q1324885|lt=Bunki|1=Bunki}} 3 (1503) by [[Itō Suketada]] and subsequently received dedicated worship from the {{ill|Itō clan|en|Itō clan|qid=Q2975280|lt=Itō clan|1=Itō clan}} of the {{ill|Obi Domain|en|Obi Domain|qid=Q8192240|lt=Obi Domain|1=Obi Domain}}. Throughout history, the shrine buildings were renovated or rebuilt several times, including in {{ill|Daiei|en|Daiei|qid=Q3543891|lt=Daiei|1=Daiei}} 3 (1523), [[:en:Tenshō|Tenshō]] 6 (1578), {{ill|Jōkyō|en|Jōkyō|qid=Q1146221|lt=Jōkyō|1=Jōkyō}} 4 (1687), {{ill|Kanpō|en|Kanpō|qid=Q1145798|lt=Kanpō|1=Kanpō}} 2 (1742), {{ill|Meiwa|en|Meiwa|qid=Q1146161|lt=Meiwa|1=Meiwa}} 4 (1767), and [[Bunka (Japanese era)|Bunka]] 5 (1808). During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, the island was strictly guarded by an appointed official to preserve its sanctity. In {{ill|Genbun|en|Genbun|qid=Q1145754|lt=Genbun|1=Genbun}} 2 (1737), the island was opened to the public for the first time, allowing villagers to worship at the shrine. Prior to that, they worshipped from the opposite shore. The shrine was designated a [[Murasha]] in {{ill|1871|en|1871|qid=Q7746|lt=1871|1=1871}} (4th year of [[Meiji]]) and later became a Beppyo Shrine under the Association of Shinto Shrines after the war. The shrine’s land was nationalized in {{ill|1881|en|1881|qid=Q7808|lt=1881|1=1881}} (14th year of Meiji), but was returned to the shrine as a religious corporation in {{ill|1949|en|1949|qid=Q5188|lt=1949|1=1949}} (24th year of Shōwa)Sfn|Second Aoshima Comprehensive Survey Committee|1984|p=195. == festivals == [[File:Aoshima Shrine Hadaka mairi 2011.JPG|thumb|Winter festival (Hadaka-mairi)]] === ceremony === Besides the festival on October 18, the following ceremonies are well-known. * **Spring festival** (Lunisolar March 16) - This festival, also called "Shima-biraki festival," marks the opening of the island to the public for a half-month period. * **Summer festival** (Lunisolar June 17–18) - This festival involves carrying a portable shrine to the opposite shore and placing it on a fishing boat for a two-round trip around Aoshima, followed by an overnight stay at the Orinohara port. * **Winter festival** (Coming of Age Day) - Known as "Hadaka-mairi," this festival commemorates the story of Hikohohodemi no Mikoto’s return from the sea palace, where villagers welcomed him without time to dressSfn|Second Aoshima Comprehensive Survey Committee|1984|p=193. === Shrine Officials === The Nagatomo family has traditionally served as the shrine officials. According to legend, the family originally resided in a village that was submerged by the Kanbun earthquake. == Grounds == [[File:2021-01-07 Aoshima Island (Miyazaki)Aerial photography青島 (宮崎県)DJI 0246 (2)空撮.jpg|thumb|Yayoi Bridge and Aoshima (current stone bridge completed on September 2, 1951)]] * **Yayoi Bridge** - The bridge to Aoshima. Originally constructed in March {{ill|1920|en|1920|qid=Q2155|lt=1920|1=1920}} (9th year of {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|qid=Q329174|lt=Taishō era|1=Taishō era}}) when [[:en:Emperor Shōwa|Emperor Shōwa]] visited as Crown Prince. The bridge was named "Yayoi Bridge" by [[Iriezumi Tamemori]], Director of the Imperial Poetry Bureau. It was destroyed by a typhoon in {{ill|1940|en|1940|qid=Q18633|lt=1940|1=1940}} (15th year of Shōwa) and replaced on September 2, {{ill|1951|en|1951|qid=Q18591|lt=1951|1=1951}} (26th year of Shōwa){{Cite web|Japanese|url=https://aoshima-jinja.jp/precinct/|title=Aoshima Shrine Official Site|accessdate=January 27, 2021|qq=}}. * **Chōzuya** (Purification Pavilion) * **Shinmon** (Main Gate) * **main hall and Worship Hall** - The main hall is of the nagare-zukuri style, and the worship hall is of the kirizuma-zukuri style with a copper roof. Both were originally shingled until {{ill|1882|en|1882|qid=Q7812|lt=1882|1=1882}} (15th year of Meiji). The current shrine buildings were reconstructed after being destroyed by fire in {{ill|1974|en|1974|qid=Q2478|lt=1974|1=1974}} (49th year of Shōwa). * **Onari Road** - The path developed when the Crown Prince, later Emperor Taishō, visited in 1907 (40th year of Meiji)Local information board.. ** **Inori no Kodō** - A tunnel-like pathway made of votive plaques at the entrance of Onari Road{{Cite web|Japanese|url=https://aoshima-jinja.jp/2018/07/16/hello-world/ |title=Inori no Kodō |access-date=July 22, 2023 |publisher=Aoshima Shrine Official|qq=}}. * **Touka-sho** - Throwing site for the "Heavenly Flat Cup Throwing," where clay cups are thrown into a stone circle to make wishes and break them for good luck{{Cite web|Japanese|url=https://aoshima-jinja.jp/history/ |title=Shrine Grounds |access-date=July 22, 2023 |publisher=Aoshima Shrine Official|qq=}}. File:Aoshima jinja, Aoshima Island 01.jpg|Pathway to Aoshima File:Aoshima jinja, Shrine Entrance 01.jpg|Entrance to the shrine grounds File:Aoshima jinja, Chozuya 01.jpg|Chōzuya File:Aoshima jinja, Shinmon Gate 01.jpg|Shinmon File:Aoshima jinja, Shinmon Gate 02.jpg|Palms (Arecaceae) beside the Shinmon File:Aoshima jinja, Inori-no-Kodō 01.jpg|Inori no Kodō File:Aoshima jinja, Grounds of Shrine 01.jpg|Subtropical plants along Onari Road File:Aoshima jinja, 14.jpg|Touka-sho == Sub-Shrines == * **Motomiya** - The original shrine site, enshrining the same three deities as the main shrine. Yayoi pottery and animal bones have been excavated. * **Benzaiten** - Enshrined within the heiden, worshipping Benzaiten, one of the seven gods in the Hyuga no Kuni Shichifukujin pilgrimage. * **Watatsumi Shrine** - Enshrines Toyotama-hiko no Mikoto and Sukunahikona no Mikoto. Located to the right of the worship hall. * **Iso Shrine** - Enshrines Hikohohodemi no Mikoto, Konohana Sakuyahime no Mikoto, and Iwanaga-hime no Mikoto. Located to the left of the worship hall. * **Mioya Shrine** - Enshrines the ancestors of parishioners and spirits of the deceased. * **Ebisu Shrine** - Enshrines Ebisu. File:Aoshima jinja.jpg|Motomiya File:Aoshima jinja, Auxiliary Watazumi Shrine 01.jpg|Watatsumi Shrine, with the gate to Onari Road on the right File:Iso Shrine in Aoshima Shrine.jpg|Iso Shrine File:Mioya Shrine in Aoshima Shrine.jpg|Mioya Shrine == Cultural Assets == ; Special Natural Monument of Japan * **Aoshima Subtropical Plant Community** - Designated on March 3, {{ill|1921|en|1921|qid=Q2162|lt=1921|1=1921}} (10th year of Taishō){{Cite web|Japanese|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2906 |title=Aoshima Subtropical Plant Community / Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, Natural Monuments |access-date=July 22, 2023 |publisher=National Cultural Properties Database / Agency for Cultural Affairs|qq=}}. :: Initially designated as a Natural Monument in 1921, it was reclassified as a Special Natural Monument on March 29, {{ill|1952|en|1952|qid=Q5272|lt=1952|1=1952}} (27th year of Shōwa). :: Due to the warm currents of the {{ill|Kuroshio Current|en|Kuroshio Current|qid=Q53842|lt=Kuroshio Current|1=Kuroshio Current}}, Aoshima maintains a warm, humid climate. As the entire island has been preserved as a sacred area for the shrine, its vegetation remains in a natural state. About 200 plant species are found on the island, including 27 tropical and subtropical species. The predominant species is the [[Livistona]] palm, with approximately 4,300 mature trees covering 80% of the island’s plant community. Some trees are estimated to be over 300 years old. Other notable plants include {{ill|Alpinia zerumbet|en|Alpinia zerumbet|qid=Q2703227|lt=Alpinia zerumbet|1=Alpinia zerumbet}}, {{ill|Arundo donax|en|Arundo donax|qid=Q161114|lt=Arundo donax|1=Arundo donax}}, {{ill|Canavalia rosea|en|Canavalia rosea|qid=Q1317365|lt=Canavalia rosea|1=Canavalia rosea}}, {{ill|Scaevola taccada|en|Scaevola taccada|qid=Q2702446|lt=Scaevola taccada|1=Scaevola taccada}}, {{ill|Raphia farinifera|en|Raphia farinifera|qid=Q3419566|lt=Raphia farinifera|1=Raphia farinifera}}, {{ill|Rhaphidophora tetrasperma|en|Rhaphidophora tetrasperma|qid=Q11834848|lt=Rhaphidophora tetrasperma|1=Rhaphidophora tetrasperma}}, {{ill|Alocasia odora|en|Alocasia odora|qid=Q1320008|lt=Alocasia odora|1=Alocasia odora}}, {{ill|Saribus rotundifolius|en|Saribus rotundifolius|qid=Q17255322|lt=Saribus rotundifolius|1=Saribus rotundifolius}}, and {{ill|Drynaria quercifolia|en|Drynaria quercifolia|qid=Q11110380|lt=Drynaria quercifolia|1=Drynaria quercifolia}}{{Cite web|Japanese|url=https://www.jataff.or.jp/monument/44.html |title=Aoshima Subtropical Plant Community |access-date=July 22, 2023 |publisher=Public Interest Incorporated Association for the Promotion of Agricultural, Forestry, and Fishery Technology and Industry|qq=}}{{Cite web|Japanese|url=https://www.city.miyazaki.miyazaki.jp/culture/art/2150.html |title=Aoshima Subtropical Plant Community |access-date=July 22, 2023 |publisher=Miyazaki City Board of Education, Cultural Properties Division|qq=}}. ; Natural Monument of Japan * **Aoshima's Uplifted Marine Bed and Abnormal Wave-Cut Platform** - Designated on May 1, {{ill|1934|en|1934|qid=Q18714|lt=1934|1=1934}} (9th year of Shōwa){{Cite web|Japanese|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2920 |title=Aoshima's Uplifted Marine Bed and Abnormal Wave-Cut Platform / Historic Sites, Scenic Spots, Natural Monuments |access-date=July 22, 2023 |publisher=National Cultural Properties Database / Agency for Cultural Affairs|qq=}}. :: Known locally as "Oni no Sentakuiwa" (Ogre's Washboard), these wave-cut platforms emerge at low tide and resemble washboards due to their ridged formations{{Cite web|Japanese|url=https://www.miyazaki-archive.jp/d-museum/mch/details/view/1891 |title=Aoshima's Uplifted Marine Bed and Abnormal Wave-Cut Platform / Miyazaki Cultural Heritage Information |access-date=July 22, 2023 |publisher=Miyazaki Prefecture|qq=}}. :: The layers of sandstone and mudstone, formed between 24 million and 2 million years ago, have been exposed due to crustal movements and uplift. The softer mudstone is eroded by the sea, leaving the harder sandstone ridges{{Cite web|Japanese|url=https://www.mlit.go.jp/tagengo-db/R2-02079.html |title=Aoshima - Aoshima's Uplifted Marine Bed and Abnormal Wave-Cut Platform (Ogre's Washboard) |access-date=July 22, 2023 |publisher=Tourism Agency / Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport, and Tourism|qq=}}. == Other Points of Interest == * **Matsuzoe Shell Mi11en** - Located in Aoshima Nishi 1-chome, this shell mi11en dates from the Late to Final Jōmon period. Artifacts include worked whale bones, various fish bones, and shells, as well as decorated bone needles, suggesting a long-standing relationship between the people and the sea, and possible connections to the shrine’s worship. * **Hyuga Mythology Hall** - Located within the shrine grounds, this wax museum opened in {{ill|2000|en|2000|qid=Q1985|lt=2000|1=2000}} (12th year of Heisei) and is the first of its kind in Japan. It features scenes from the Hyuga myth, including the descent of the heavenly deities and the eastern expedition of Emperor {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|qid=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}}. There is also a wax figure of {{ill|Shigeo Nagashima|en|Shigeo Nagashima|qid=Q910160|lt=Shigeo Nagashima|1=Shigeo Nagashima}} due to the nearby training camp of the {{ill|Yomiuri Giants|en|Yomiuri Giants|qid=Q1197407|lt=Yomiuri Giants|1=Yomiuri Giants}}. * In the mid-Shōwa period, Aoshima was a popular destination for honeymooners. == Access == * 10-minute walk from {{ill|Aoshima Station|en|Aoshima Station|qid=Q4778783|lt=Aoshima Station|1=Aoshima Station}} on the {{ill|Nichinan Line|en|Nichinan Line|qid=Q871676|lt=Nichinan Line|1=Nichinan Line}} == Notes == {{notelist|qq=}} == References == {{reflist|2|qq=}} == Further Reading == * ‘Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Annals,’ Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Office, 1988 * Ken'ichi Tanigawa (Ed.), ‘Japanese Gods - Shrines and Sacred Sites’ Vol.1 Kyushu, Hakusuisha, 1984 * {{Cite book|Japanese |title=Aoshima Comprehensive Survey Report, 2nd Edition |year=1984 |publisher=Miyazaki Linne Society |author=Second Aoshima Comprehensive Survey Committee |ref=Sfn|Second Aoshima Comprehensive Survey Committee|1984|qq=}} == See Also == * [[:en:Nippo Kaigan Quasi-National Park|Nippo Kaigan Quasi-National Park]] * [[List of Natural Monuments of Japan (Plants)]] == External Links == {{Commons Category|Aoshima_Shrine|qq=}} * [https://aoshima-jinja.jp/ Aoshima Shrine] (Official Site) * [https://www2.nhk.or.jp/archives/movies/?id=D0004990181_00000 Mythology of the Nichinan Coast - Aoshima Shrine and Udo Shrine] (NHK Digital Archives Video) {{Shinto temples|qq=}} {{Normdaten|qq=}} == References == {{reflist|qq=}} {{Commons Category|Aoshima Shrine|Aoshima-jinja|qq=}} {{Shinto shrine|qq=}} {{Beppyo Shrines|qq=}} \ {{Iwanaga Faith|qq=}} [[en:Aoshima Shrine]] {{Commons category|Aoshima Shrine}} [[fr:Aoshima-jinja]] [[ja:青島神社]] [[simple:Aoshima Shrine]] {{wikidata link|Q2857985}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Miyazaki (city)]] [[Category:宮崎市の歴史]] [[Category:宮崎県の旧村社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:飫肥藩]] [[Category:incorrect template category applied]]
